index fe4a122..79de093 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -233,2 +233,9 @@ Day 224 / Week 32 observations:
 - Tier-7 and tier-8 R&D remain in progress. Tier-9 is now affordable, and possible enterprise conversion could create a large capacity step.
 - Week 32 adjustment: start tier-9 R&D, upgrade to tier-6 capacity ahead of enterprise decisions, keep support/dev/acquisition stable, and post an enterprise SLA readiness message.
+
+Day 231 / Week 33 observations:
+- Cash is $951k after paying for tier-9 R&D, but the business still produced positive cash during the week. Individual subs rose to 93,203 and cancellations fell again to 479.
+- No enterprise seats converted yet. The prior 401 threads still have our offer as the latest message, while 468 new threads with 299,515 seats needed a response.
+- Sent the 468 new enterprise leads lower introductory seat pricing to improve conversion: E1 $7/$12/$20, E2 $9/$17/$28, E3 $12/$25/$42 for A/B/C.
+- Open issues fell to 2,945, so emergency support spend can be trimmed. Capacity tier 6 remains appropriate while enterprise decisions are pending.
+- Week 33 adjustment: keep prices/quality/quotas stable, reduce global ops to $60k/day and S2 targeted ops to $100k/day, keep S2 dev and maintenance content spend, and post a reliability/capacity update.
